annotations.label: Boolean algebra (as complemented distributive lattice)

anchor.snippet: Boolean lattice is a complemented distributive lattice

annotations.note: Mathlib constructs a `BooleanAlgebra` from any complemented bounded distributive lattice, witnessing the equivalence with its complement-operator definition.

annotations.label: Boolean algebra–Boolean ring correspondence

anchor.snippet: gives rise to a Boolean ring , and vice versa

annotations.note: Both directions exist: `BooleanAlgebra.toBooleanRing` and `BooleanRing.toBooleanAlgebra`.

annotations.label: Boolean algebra (axiomatic definition)

anchor.snippet: equipped with two binary operations

annotations.note: The `BooleanAlgebra` class axiomatizes ⊓/⊔/ᶜ (additionally bundling sdiff and Heyting implication).

annotations.label: Absorption and associativity are derivable

anchor.snippet: can be excluded from the set of axioms as they can be derived

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Lattice.mk'` assumes absorption rather than deriving it, and no result derives associativity/absorption from a reduced Huntington-style axiom set.

annotations.label: Trivial (degenerate) Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: with only one element is called a trivial Boolean algebra

annotations.note: `PUnit` carries the one-element Boolean algebra instance.

annotations.label: Equivalent meet/join order conditions

anchor.snippet: It follows from the last three pairs of axioms above

annotations.note: `inf_eq_left : a ⊓ b = a ↔ a ≤ b` and `sup_eq_right : a ⊔ b = b ↔ a ≤ b` give the order characterizations.

annotations.label: Induced order is a bounded partial order

anchor.snippet: is a partial order with least element 0 and greatest element 1

annotations.note: A Boolean algebra inherits a partial order (via `DistribLattice`) and `toBoundedOrder` supplies ⊥/⊤ as least/greatest elements.

annotations.label: First four axiom pairs define a bounded lattice

anchor.snippet: constitute a definition of a bounded lattice

annotations.note: `Lattice.mk'` builds a lattice from commutativity/associativity/absorption, but no single constructor bundles the identity (bounded) axioms into the algebraic-to-bounded-lattice equivalence.

annotations.label: Uniqueness of complements

anchor.snippet: that any complement is unique

annotations.note: `compl_unique : a ⊓ b = ⊥ → a ⊔ b = ⊤ → aᶜ = b` proves complements are unique.

annotations.label: Self-duality of the axioms

anchor.snippet: The set of axioms is self-dual in the sense that

annotations.note: The order dual of a Boolean algebra is itself a Boolean algebra, encoding the self-duality of the axioms.

annotations.label: Two-element Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: the two-element Boolean algebra , has only two elements

annotations.note: `Bool` (and `Prop`) carry the two-element Boolean algebra instance.

annotations.label: Two-element algebra decides Boolean identities

anchor.snippet: is generally true in all Boolean algebras if and only if it is true in the two-element Boolean algebra

annotations.note: The completeness metatheorem reducing all Boolean identities to the two-element algebra is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Consensus theorems

anchor.snippet: the following laws ( Consensus theorems ) are generally valid in all Boolean algebras

annotations.note: The named consensus identities are not present as lemmas in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Power set Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: forms a Boolean algebra, an algebra of sets

annotations.note: `Set α` carries a Boolean algebra (in fact complete atomic) instance under ∪/∩/ᶜ.

annotations.label: Power set of two atoms

anchor.snippet: the simplest Boolean algebra is that defined by the power set of two atoms

annotations.note: The four-element algebra arises as `Set` of a two-element type via the general powerset instance, but there is no dedicated declaration for it.

annotations.label: Finite–cofinite algebra

anchor.snippet: called the finite–cofinite algebra

annotations.note: The finite–cofinite Boolean algebra of subsets is not constructed in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Lindenbaum (free) Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: form the Lindenbaum algebra

annotations.note: No free/Lindenbaum Boolean algebra construction exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Interval algebra

anchor.snippet: the interval algebra is the smallest Boolean algebra of subsets

annotations.note: The interval Boolean algebra of half-open intervals is not formalized.

annotations.label: Divisors of n

anchor.snippet: the set of all positive divisors of

annotations.note: `Nat.divisors n` exists as a finset, but its divisibility lattice/Boolean-algebra structure is not developed.

annotations.label: Divisor lattice is Boolean iff n square-free

anchor.snippet: is a Boolean algebra if and only if n is square-free

annotations.note: No result characterizing when the divisor lattice is Boolean via square-freeness was found.

annotations.label: Clopen sets of a topological space

anchor.snippet: arise from topological spaces

annotations.note: `TopologicalSpace.Clopens α` (clopen sets) carries a `BooleanAlgebra` instance.

annotations.label: Central idempotents of a ring

anchor.snippet: its set of central idempotents

annotations.note: For a commutative ring `R`, `{a : R // IsIdempotentElem a}` is a Boolean algebra (all idempotents being central); the general noncommutative central-idempotent case is not covered.

annotations.label: Boolean algebra homomorphism

anchor.snippet: A homomorphism between two Boolean algebras

annotations.note: `BoundedLatticeHom` preserves ⊥, ⊤, ⊔, ⊓ and is the morphism type in the `BoolAlg` category.

annotations.label: Homomorphisms preserve complement

anchor.snippet: It then follows that

annotations.note: `map_compl'` shows a bounded lattice hom between Boolean algebras satisfies `f aᶜ = (f a)ᶜ`.

annotations.label: Boolean algebra isomorphism

anchor.snippet: An isomorphism between two Boolean algebras

annotations.note: Order isomorphisms `≃o` between Boolean algebras are exactly Boolean algebra isomorphisms (and isos in the `BoolAlg` category).

annotations.label: Isomorphism iff bijective

anchor.snippet: is an isomorphism if and only if it is bijective

annotations.note: `Equiv.toOrderIso` still requires the inverse to be monotone; no lemma states that a bijective Boolean-algebra homomorphism is automatically an isomorphism.

annotations.label: Boolean algebra yields a Boolean ring

anchor.snippet: rings with this property are called Boolean rings

annotations.note: `BooleanAlgebra.toBooleanRing` builds a Boolean ring with + = symmDiff and * = ⊓.

annotations.label: Boolean ring yields a Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: we can turn it into a Boolean algebra

annotations.note: `BooleanRing.toBooleanAlgebra` builds a Boolean algebra with a ⊔ b = a+b+a*b and a ⊓ b = a*b.

annotations.label: Equivalence of Boolean ring and Boolean algebra categories

anchor.snippet: The categories of Boolean rings and Boolean algebras are equivalent

annotations.note: `boolRingCatEquivBoolAlg : BoolRing ≌ BoolAlg` gives the (in fact isomorphism of) categories.

annotations.label: Ideal of a Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: An ideal of the Boolean algebra

annotations.note: `Order.Ideal` (nonempty, downward-closed, upward-directed) specializes to Boolean-algebra ideals in a join-semilattice-with-bottom.

annotations.label: Prime ideal

anchor.snippet: is called prime if I ≠ A

annotations.note: `Order.Ideal.IsPrime` defines prime ideals (proper, with `x ⊓ y ∈ I → x ∈ I ∨ y ∈ I`).

annotations.label: Maximal ideal

anchor.snippet: is called maximal if I ≠ A

annotations.note: `Order.Ideal.IsMaximal` defines maximal (coatom) proper ideals.

annotations.label: Prime and maximal ideals coincide

anchor.snippet: the notions of prime ideal and maximal ideal are equivalent

annotations.note: In a Boolean algebra `IsPrime.isMaximal` holds, and `IsMaximal.isPrime` (distrib lattices) gives the converse, so the notions coincide.

annotations.label: Filter of a Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: A filter of the Boolean algebra

annotations.note: `Order.PFilter` is the order-theoretic filter (dual to `Order.Ideal`), specializing to Boolean-algebra filters.

annotations.label: Ultrafilter

anchor.snippet: ideal in a Boolean algebra is ultrafilter

annotations.note: Mathlib's `Ultrafilter` is the maximal filter on a powerset (set) Boolean algebra; general-Boolean-algebra ultrafilters appear only dually via `Order.Ideal.IsMaximal`.

annotations.label: Ultrafilter lemma

anchor.snippet: is called the ultrafilter lemma

annotations.note: `Ultrafilter.exists_le` extends any proper filter to an ultrafilter; the general-BA form is `Order.Ideal.IsProper.exists_le_maximal`.

annotations.label: Finite Boolean algebras are power set algebras

anchor.snippet: every finite Boolean algebra is isomorphic to the Boolean algebra of all subsets of a finite set

annotations.note: Birkhoff representation for finite Boolean algebras is left as an explicit TODO in `FinBoolAlg`.

annotations.label: Stone's representation theorem

anchor.snippet: Stone's celebrated representation theorem for Boolean algebras

annotations.note: The prime-ideal separation ingredient of Stone duality is proved, but the full representation theorem (BA ≅ clopens of its Stone space) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Huntington 1933 axiomatization

anchor.snippet: the following elegant axiomatization for Boolean algebra

annotations.note: Huntington's 1933 (∨, ¬) axiomatization is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Robbins algebra

anchor.snippet: Calling (1), (2), and (4) a Robbins algebra

annotations.note: No Robbins algebra structure is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every Robbins algebra is a Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: Every Robbins algebra is a Boolean algebra

annotations.note: The Robbins theorem is listed in `docs/1000.yaml` without a Mathlib declaration.

annotations.label: Generalized Boolean algebra

anchor.snippet: a distributive lattice B is a generalized Boolean lattice

annotations.note: `GeneralizedBooleanAlgebra` is a distributive lattice with ⊥ and relative complement `\`.

annotations.label: Generalized Boolean lattices are ideals of Boolean lattices

anchor.snippet: Generalized Boolean lattices are exactly the ideals of Boolean lattices

annotations.note: Although both `GeneralizedBooleanAlgebra` and `Order.Ideal` exist, the structural identification of generalized Boolean lattices with ideals of Boolean lattices is not proved.

annotations.label: Orthocomplemented lattice

anchor.snippet: is called an orthocomplemented lattice

annotations.note: No orthocomplemented (or orthomodular) lattice structure is defined in Mathlib.
